name: Lying Leg Curl
resistance_profile: Prone knee flexion; complements hip-hinge hamstring work.
execution_steps: Lie face-down on the machine with the ankle pad resting just above your heels.
Grip the handles and press your hips down into the pad.
Curl your heels toward your glutes as far as they will go.
Lower under control back to straight legs.
good_when: You want to isolate knee-flexion hamstring function to complement hip-hinge work.
You want a simple, stable movement with low fatigue cost.
You want to bias the shortened, peak-contraction end of the curl.
bad_when: You want lengthened-biased hamstring loading (a seated curl or RDL keeps the hip flexed).
Your hips keep popping off the pad (a seated curl positions you better).
